data.title: Consensus Indicators Signal A K-Shaped Recovery In Both Europe And The U.S.
data.description:
By Rich Hill, Global Head of Real Estate Research & Strategy

InREV, the European Association for Investors in Non-Listed Real Estate Vehicles, recently published their Consensus Indicator for March 2026, revealing that sentiment remains positive but is moderating amid rising geopolitical and interest rate pressures. The indicator provides quarterly insights into the dynamics of current and anticipated conditions in the economy, investment, leasing and operations, development, and new lending.

This quarterly report is especially valuable as it provides key insight into how investors are positioning for the global macro landscape that we described in our 2026 Global CRE Outlook as unusually complex and disorderly.

Against this backdrop, we argued that real estate investors should focus on what they can control: driving net operating income growth. Achieving this will require disciplined property, market, and fund selection, as return dispersion across real estate continues to widen.

What did the Consensus Indicator say?

Investor sentiment towards European real estate markets remained positive in Q1 2026 for the eighth consecutive quarter. However, optimism moderated amid concerns over the potential impacts of the conflict in the Middle East. Notably, 74% of responses were submitted after the outbreak of the conflict involving Iran.

  • The headline reading softened to 54.7 in Q1 2026, down from 59.4 in Q4 2025. Notably, the reading is higher than the 2Q 2025 reading of 52.2 in the aftermath of “Liberation Day,” and is above all readings from 1Q23 to 2Q24. For reference, a figure above 50 indicates expansion and signals optimism regarding the trajectory of the market recovery, while a reading below 50 points to contraction.
  • Two sub-indicators remained firmly in positive territory: financing conditions and leasing operations. Investment liquidity declined sharply, though it remained marginally positive. By contrast, the economic outlook and new development indicators fell sharply into negative territory, to 42.4 and 43.8, respectively.

Reinforcing a Cycle for Selectivity

The decline in the indicator is understandable, as ECB inflation forecasts for 2026 have already been revised upward by 70bps to 2.6% in March 2026, compared to December 2025, which is pressuring rates higher. Indeed, Geopolitical risks and interest rates were two of the top 5 themes that emerged in our recent discussions with European investors.

However, fundamentals remain on solid footing as longer-term lease structures continue to support income stability, and while interest rates have risen, the debt capital markets remain open and liquid. All of this reinforces our view that this is a cycle for selectivity, as there is a wide range of rent growth forecasts across European markets and property types provided by Property Market Analysis LLP (PMA).

Other measures of sentiment highlight a cycle in recovery, albeit an uneven one

A V-shaped recovery in headline valuations remains unlikely given the lack of meaningful stimulus. Instead, returns are expected to normalize gradually. While that may suggest a U-shaped path, widening dispersion in returns across property types and markets points to something closer to a K-shaped recovery.

  • The Real Estate Roundtable released its Sentiment Index for 1Q26. It showed an overall score of 66, a decrease of 1 point from the previous quarter. The Current Index registered 66, a 2-point increase over Q4 2025. The Future Index posted a score of 67 points, a decrease of 2 points from the previous quarter, reflecting a prevailing sentiment that the market is in the early stages of a tentative, uneven recovery. Political, tariff, and interest rate uncertainty is contributing to wide spreads between buyers and sellers. Amid the uncertainty around pricing clarity and geopolitical stability, participants are cautiously optimistic for an improved 2026.
  • The CRE Finance Council (CREFC), the industry association representing the $6.3 trillion U.S. commercial and multifamily real estate finance sector, released its 4Q25 Sentiment Index Survey on January 22, 2026. It showed the index rose 2.1% to 125.4, from 122.8 in 3Q 2025, approaching the all-time survey high of 126.6 set in 4Q 2024. The survey's topical questions revealed a market preparing for significant refinancing activity amid uneven outcomes.

Bottom line

Investor sentiment toward European real estate remains positive, but has moderated amid heightened geopolitical risk and rising interest rate pressures. While financing and leasing conditions remain supportive, expectations for the broader economy and new development have declined.

Overall, the data in both Europe and the U.S. point to an uneven recovery characterized by widening dispersion across markets and property types, reinforcing the need for selectivity and a focus on income growth rather than a rapid, broad-based rebound.

We believe the cycle ahead will be alpha-driven and real estate investors will increasingly need to take a page from equity markets, where asset and market selection drive performance.

Original Post

Editor's Note: The summary bullets for this article were chosen by Seeking Alpha editors.

data.title: Virtus SGA Global Growth Fund: Q4 2025 Buys, Sells, And Standouts
data.description:
The following segment was excerpted from the Virtus SGA Global Growth Fund Q4 2025 Commentary.

Largest Contributors

Alphabet (GOOGL)

Alphabet was a top contributor during the quarter, driven by accelerating growth across its core businesses and strong execution in AI-enabled products. Search queries and revenues both accelerated, challenging concerns about competitive threats from AI chat tools and supporting the case for continued innovation-driven market expansion. YouTube and Cloud also posted robust growth, with Cloud backlog and revenues benefiting from strong enterprise demand for AI solutions. Alphabet's disciplined margin management and diversified revenue streams, alongside strategic investments in AI and cloud, position it well for continued growth. Despite ongoing regulatory and competitive pressures, Alphabet's leadership in search, video, and cloud, combined with its ability to monetize a loyal global user base, should support double-digit earnings and revenue growth in the years ahead. We raised the target to an above-average weight position.

Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing (TSM)

TSMC was a top contributor during the quarter, driven by robust demand for advanced semiconductor manufacturing and improved gross margins as AI continues to grow strong and the non-AI segment showed signs of recovery. Management raised its revenue growth guidance to the mid-30% range, and given continued strength in demand, AI-related growth targets are expected to move above the current mid-40% level. TSMC's leadership in cutting-edge process nodes remains undisputed. The company raised its CapEx guidance to support future growth and is expected to increase its AI revenue growth target, reflecting confidence in the secular expansion of high-performance computing and AI workloads. Strategic steps to diversify manufacturing outside Taiwan, including new facilities in Japan, Germany, and the U.S., further strengthen TSMC's position. With its technology leadership, strong customer relationships, and disciplined execution, TSMC remains well-positioned to deliver strong double-digit revenue and earnings growth over the coming years. We raised the position target to an above-average weight during the quarter.

Fast Retailing (FRCOY)

Fast Retailing was a top contributor during the quarter, driven by strong execution across its global operations. China returned to growth in August and September, with management guiding for further improvement in both revenue and profit in the coming fiscal year, a turnaround following topline decline in China this fiscal year. International operations continued to show significant growth and margin expansion, particularly in North America and Europe, where the company continues to target good growth and further margin improvement. Fast Retailing's scale, efficient supply chain, and ability to offer quality products at attractive prices help navigate macroeconomic headwinds and currency volatility, while ongoing innovation in product design and marketing support brand strength and customer loyalty. With Japan accounting for one-third of Uniqlo's business and international regions poised for further expansion, Fast Retailing remains well positioned to capitalize on global growth opportunities across its diverse markets and deliver double-digit revenue growth in the years ahead. We maintained a below-average weight position during the quarter, trimming on strength.

Largest Detractors

Arm Holdings (ARM)

Arm Holdings was a detractor during the quarter, despite a strong fiscal Q2 earnings report with revenue up 34% and profit 43%. Royalty revenue grew 21%, driven by triple digits data center growth and higher smartphone royalty from compute subsystem (CSS) customers. License revenue increased 56%, or 17% excluding related-party design service fees from SoftBank (SFTBY). The next quarter guidance for sales growth of 25% and profit up 20% was also ahead of expectations. However, the company faced several headwinds that weighed on investor sentiment. Elevated R&D spending, mostly related to Arm's design service for SoftBank, will impact Arm's margin outlook for next year. In addition, SoftBank is borrowing against its Arm investment to finance its investment into OpenAI, which raised concerns over forced selling of Arm shares if SoftBank does not secure sufficient funding for its investment commitments. Despite these near-term challenges, we remain confident in Arm's growth outlook, and we expect Arm to provide greater clarity over its chip project with SoftBank in 2026. We maintained a below-average weight position during the quarter, adding to the position on weakness.

Microsoft (MSFT)

Microsoft, a global leader in enterprise software and cloud services, was a detractor during the quarter, despite an overall solid quarterly report. Azure growth of 39% came in slightly below expectations, and guidance for the next quarter was weaker than expected, which management asserted was due to supply constraints.

The company's partnership with OpenAI has accelerated investments in infrastructure and the ultimate return on these investments remains difficult to quantify. At the same time, the adoption of Copilot has been lackluster, and Microsoft remains dependent on OpenAI for the core AI features. In our view, the returns on the infrastructure investments should be exciting over time. We also remain confident that Microsoft can develop the LLM expertise in-house or partner with other companies as OpenAI is unlikely to maintain the level of dominance it has had thus far in the AI journey.

Microsoft's business model is anchored by substantial pricing power, high switching costs, and a growing base of recurring revenues as more products transition to cloud and Software-as-a-Service models. While elevated capital expenditures and evolving market dynamics present challenges, Microsoft's strong customer base, leadership in cloud and AI, and disciplined execution position it well for double-digit earnings and revenue growth as the benefits of these investments materialize. We trimmed the position during the quarter, maintaining an above-average weight.

MercadoLibre (MELI)

MercadoLibre was a detractor during the quarter amid continued macroeconomic and competitive pressures across its e-commerce and fintech segments. Third quarter results were mixed with revenue growth of 39% (49% in constant currency) slightly ahead of expectations driven by Brazil and Mexico while Argentina, which accounts for a substantial portion of profits, was weak due to ongoing macroeconomic pressures. Units sold accelerated to 39% from 31%, helped by lower free shipping thresholds, while shipping costs declined due to more efficient use of excess capacity. However, margins were a bit light, reflecting the impact of free shipping initiatives. In Brazil and Mexico, intensifying competition from global and regional rivals required continued investment in faster delivery, loyalty benefits, and seller support, pressuring unit economics. Credit performance was solid, but net interest margins contracted due to higher funding costs, especially in Argentina. Management remains confident in the company's market leadership, logistics, and broad fintech offerings, and is investing in demand drivers rather than cutting merchant fees like competitors. As the regional leader in both e-commerce and fintech, MercadoLibre benefits from self-reinforcing scale advantages across its interlocking platforms, which should support margin improvement progression despite intensifying competition in the near-term. MercadoLibre's scaled logistics network, expanding advertising monetization, and the region's low e-commerce and fintech penetration position the company well for attractive low-20s earnings and revenue growth trajectories over the next three years. We maintained a below-average weight position.

Portfolio Activity

We initiated new positions in Broadcom (AVGO), Netflix (NFLX), and Sea Limited (SE). Two positions were liquidated during the quarter, ServiceNow (NOW) and Infosys (INFY). In the fourth quarter, several positions were added to on weakness including Adyen (ADYEY), Amazon (AMZN), Arm Holdings, Nvidia (NVDA), and Salesforce (CRM). Positions in Fast Retailing, HDFC Bank (HDB), and Microsoft among others were trimmed on relative strength to fund compelling opportunities elsewhere.

Purchases

Broadcom

During the quarter, we initiated a below-average weight position in leading semiconductor and infrastructure software company Broadcom. Broadcom's ongoing transformation from a provider of custom ASICs to a full rack-scale solutions vendor, including scale-up networking, marks a significant improvement in its strategic positioning compared to earlier in the year. This strategic evolution has been validated by recent product launches, such as the Scale-up Ethernet solution, and committed orders from major customers like Anthropic. Broadcom is also diversifying its customer base, expanding from three to five qualified customers with purchase commitments, and accelerating production volume timelines for future prospective clients. The shift in AI demand from training to inferencing is creating more visible and stable revenue streams, as inferencing workloads have grown rapidly, driven by innovations in long context windows and agentic workloads.

Broadcom's business model is supported by strong pricing power, recurring revenues, and robust growth opportunities. The company maintains industry-leading margins, with semiconductor gross margin around 70% and software gross margin near 90%, leveraging high technology barriers and strong customer relationships. Recurring revenue is underpinned by long-term AI semiconductor engagements and high-retention infrastructure software subscriptions. As AI spending shifts toward inference, revenue visibility and stability improve, backed by real use cases and multi-year customer commitments. Broadcom's growth is driven by rising AI demand, as major labs and hyperscalers adopt custom ASICs for both training and inference. The company's ability to integrate a full systems solution from ASICs to networking provides an advantage over other vendors, and its strategy of building sustainable franchises ensures resilience against market and technology shifts. While competition and customer concentration remain risks, Broadcom is actively expanding its custom ASIC engagements to improve diversification. Broadcom's strategic evolution and focus on organic growth and AI position it well for long-term stability and profitability, supporting over 20% revenue and earnings growth.

Netflix

During the quarter, we initiated a new position in Netflix, the global leader in streaming entertainment. Netflix leverages its unrivaled scale and content library to exercise consistent pricing power while maintaining industry-low churn rates. The company's recurring subscription model, supported by a highly engaged and loyal user base, generates stable and predictable cash flows. Netflix is well-positioned to capture significant long-term growth as connected TV adoption rises and content offerings expand across geographies. The company's ability to personalize recommendations, invest in original programming, and scale new initiatives such as ad-supported tiers and gaming further enhances its competitive moat and monetization potential. Netflix's shares were impacted by the uncertainty over the pending Warner Bros. Discovery (WBD) acquisition. If the company prevails and closes the acquisition, we expect Netflix to leverage Warner's deep IPs library to broaden engagement and global growth and drive meaningful synergies. On the other hand, if the deal falls through, we do not expect meaningful changes to the competitive landscape. Regardless of the outcome of the deal, we believe Netflix is well-positioned to gain further share of global TV viewing, drive margin accretion, and sustain strong double-digit long-term earnings growth as it continues to innovate in content, technology, and distribution. We plan to build the position opportunistically moving forward.

Sea Limited

During the quarter, we initiated a new position in Sea Limited, a Southeast Asian consumer internet company with an integrated ecosystem combining e-commerce, digital payments, and entertainment. Sea has a diversified business model, with its Shopee e-commerce platform, a mobile-centric marketplace that provides integrated payments, logistics infrastructure, and seller services; Garena, a digital entertainment platform for users to access mobile and PC online games; and Monee digital financial services offering credit, mobile wallets, payment processing, banking, and insurtech services. Sea operates in markets with unique geopolitical, cultural, and economic complexities, and the company is ahead in localizing its products and content, making it difficult for competitors to disrupt at scale. Sea's e-commerce business benefits from low penetration rates and rising digital adoption, while its logistics network and localized approach create meaningful competitive advantages that are difficult for competitors to replicate. The company's digital financial services segment is scaling rapidly, leveraging accumulated user data and e-commerce use cases to drive engagement and profitability. In gaming, Sea's deep understanding of local audiences and self-developed franchises like Free Fire are favorable for emerging markets audiences where low-end smartphones dominate. While near-term margin volatility is expected as Sea invests in logistics, fulfillment, and new initiatives such as free shipping membership, management's focus on building long-term competitive advantages and expanding addressable markets positions the company well for sustainable growth.

Among the key risk considerations are increasing competition from TikTok and Lazada (backed by Alibaba (BABA)) in Southeast Asia, MercadoLibre in Brazil, and Coupang (CPNG) in Taiwan. TikTok has been able to rapidly grow market share leveraging its user traffic on social media and the market share dynamic between Shopee, TikTok, and potential new entrants needs to be closely considered. We are also monitoring the unique political, macroeconomic, and currency risks across the multiple markets Sea operates in. Additionally, a potential slowdown in the U.S. and China could weaken the region's economic development and lead to depreciation in local currencies. We believe the company is positioned to benefit from low market penetration and an above average GDP growth rate across its geographies.

Profitability and positive free cash flow are growing strongly supported by the company's earlier investments, which we expect will continue to drive repeatable revenues from a large installed base. We plan to build the position opportunistically moving forward.

Sales

ServiceNow

During the quarter, we liquidated our position in ServiceNow due to forced attrition. While ServiceNow remains a well-positioned leader in enterprise workflow automation, recent developments have increased uncertainty about prospects for the share price. Execution remains strong, but concerns have grown regarding a potential deceleration in 2026 organic growth, a higher valuation multiple, and a shift in strategy from disciplined organic expansion to larger, more aggressive M&A, highlighted by the recently announced acquisition of Armis. This change could introduce greater integration risk and alter the company's historically strong execution profile. Given these factors, we chose to reallocate capital to higher conviction opportunities with clearer visibility into durable growth.

Infosys

We liquidated our position in Infosys during the quarter due to forced attrition. More recently, investor sentiment toward India has cooled, driven by softening domestic demand, persistent tariff uncertainty, and a rotation of foreign capital into markets more exposed to AI and semiconductor growth. Along with other outsourced IT service providers, Infosys' shares have been weak as concerns grew over the potential for these business models to be disrupted by AI, amid a softer demand environment and a change in enterprise spending priorities. While we believe IT services providers remain well positioned to assist with enterprise AI deployment, we had more attractive opportunities in the portfolio to reallocate capital to.

The opinions expressed herein reflect the opinions of Sustainable Growth Advisers, LP (VRTS) and are subject to change without notice. Past performance is no guarantee for future results. This information is supplemental and complements a GIPS Report that can be found with composite performance. The securities referenced in the article are not a solicitation or recommendation to buy, sell or hold securities. This commentary is provided only for qualified and sophisticated institutional investors.

Results are presented gross and net of management fees and include the reinvestment of all income. For interest and capital gains, SGA does not withhold taxes. For dividends, SGA will withhold taxes as reported by the client's custodian. Returns are calculated net of withholding taxes on dividends. The Net Returns are calculated based on the deduction of a model fee of 3.00% being the highest applicable fee that may be charged to SGA clients for the Global Growth WRAP strategy. Net Returns do account for custodian and brokerage fees. Actual fees charged to clients may vary depending on, among other things, the applicable fees schedule and portfolio size. SGA's fees are available upon request and may be found in Part 2A of its Form ADV. The largest contributors and detractors are determined using a ranking of the absolute contribution to portfolio return by each security held over the period under consideration. Policies for valuing investments, calculating performance, and preparing GIPS Reports are available upon request. Upon request, free of charge, SGA can provide a list of all portfolio holdings held in SGA's Global Growth WRAP portfolio for the past year. SGA's earnings growth forecast data is based upon portfolio companies' non-GAAP operating earnings.Sustainable Growth Advisers, LP ("SGA") was formed in 2003 and is a registered investment advisor under the Investment Advisers Act of 1940. SGA manages portfolios of publicly traded equity assets according to its "Large Cap Growth Equity" investment approach for pooled funds, institutions, trusts and private accounts. SGA is an operationally independent investment management firm that and is an affiliate of Virtus Investment Partners (VRTS). The SGA Global Growth WRAP Composite was created in September 2019. The firm maintains a complete list and description of all composites, which is available upon request.

data.title: Cognyte Software: Compelling EBITDA, Low Dilution
data.description:
As 2026 drags on, investors continue to be incredibly wary of investing in tech stocks again, as the combination of a weak macro environment plus fears of a "SaaSpocalypse" in which AI uproots existing software platforms, continues to drive a wholesale rotation out of software stocks.

These conditions make it perfect for long-term-oriented investors to pick up shares of attractive businesses that are just beginning to generate meaningful profitability. In my view, the combination of recurring revenue, solid growth indicators that defy the notion of any near-term AI disruption, and bottom-line progress that can't be ignored will help to drive an upward re-rating in stocks like Cognyte Software (CGNT), the vertical software platform that targets public sector agencies. Cognyte is down slightly since the start of the year, but a very strong Q4 earnings print (which drove ~7% upside in the stock) has helped to offset recent losses. In my view, this is the start of a longer rebound rally in this name.

I last wrote a "Buy" article on Cognyte in December, when the stock was trading near $10 per share. Since then, shares of Cognyte have fallen ~15%. While I agree that my previous buy call was ill-timed, when I take a fresh look at the company now, I find a lot to like: a flowering adjusted EBITDA profile, strong billings and backlog growth, and low dilution, unlike many of its peers in the SaaS sector. I reiterate my "Buy" rating here.

Of course, we should kick off the discussion with an important question: Is Cognyte a disruptor in AI, or is it about to be disrupted? That important question is the single factor that is driving the market for software stocks today. As a refresher, consider what Cognyte does. The company targets primarily public sector agencies (law enforcement, national security, and financial crimes investigations) to ingest reams of big data, helping to drive intelligent decisions, understand and identify threats, and detect vulnerabilities.

There is likely a lot of pressure in these agencies to adopt AI to speed up workflows and reduce human labor. At the same time, it's difficult to imagine various public sector agencies developing their own AI tools in-house. The far more likely scenario is that they engage a provider like Cognyte to deliver a professionally built, proven platform to achieve these aims.

We've seen no evidence that Cognyte is at any risk of being displaced by Vibe coding or AI, and yet its valuation multiples essentially suggest this doomsday scenario. At current share prices near $8, Cognyte trades at a $617.2 million market cap. After we net off the $116.9 million of cash on Cognyte's most recent balance sheet, the company's resulting enterprise value is $500.3 million.

As shown in the chart above, for FY27 (the year for Cognyte ending in July 2027), the company is guiding to $448 million in revenue (12% y/y growth) and $68 million in adjusted EBITDA (a 15.2% margin and 40% y/y growth).

We note that Cognyte has also maintained its medium-term outlook, which calls for at least $500 million in revenue at a >20% adjusted EBITDA margin by FY28, which implies a further 47% y/y growth in adjusted EBITDA in FY28 to $100 million. This positions Cognyte's valuation multiples at:

  • 1.1x EV/FY27 revenue and 1.0x EV/FY28 revenue
  • 7.4x EV/FY27 adjusted EBITDA and 5.0x EV/FY28 adjusted EBITDA

No, Cognyte is not the most exciting or fastest-growing software company, but its reasonable and consistent growth rates, plus the lack of any evidence for any AI disruption on top of ongoing margin performance, make this a stock worth buying on the dip.

Q4 download

Let's now run through Cognyte's latest quarterly results in greater detail. The Q4 earnings summary is shown below:

Cognyte's revenue grew 12% y/y to $106.2 million, which largely kept pace with the 13% y/y growth in Q3. Where I am impressed, however, is in the company's other top-line metrics. As a reminder, billings is often one of the best indicators of a recurring-revenue software company's longer-term growth trajectory, as it captures deals signed in the quarter that will be recognized as revenue in future quarters.

In Q4, Cognyte achieved $109.9 million in billings, which grew 15.7% y/y. The fact that billings growth outpaced revenue growth is a strong signal that Cognyte's revenue growth isn't set to decelerate from here. It's also a good signal that Cognyte will be able to hit its 12% revenue growth target for FY27.

Also, we note that the company's short-term RPO grew 10% y/y to $369.5 million. Recall that the short-term portion of RPO, or essentially backlog, is contractually committed deals that will be recognized as revenue within the next 12 months. This backlog already represents 83% of the company's $448 million revenue target for FY27, which indicates a good deal of room for upside.

The company notes that momentum in securing new logos is strong. Per CEO Elad Sharon's remarks on the Q4 earnings call:

Second, new logos. We added 61 new customers this year as our solutions continue to prove themselves globally and deliver real operational value. This important new business is driven by our proven track record and customer references and is aligned with our land and expand strategy. We increased our footprint within military intelligence agencies, including in NATO countries.

And third, North America. This is a key market for us. We recently strengthened our North American leadership, bringing in a seasoned sales executive with deep experience and track record in the federal market. We also added a new channel partner, Carahsoft, who will provide access to federal, state and local procurement channels and will support broader deployments of our solutions. Together, these steps reinforce our commitment to scaling our U.S. presence and aligning with long-term federal modernization programs.

Our growth is driven by a balanced approach, installed base expansion, new customer acquisition and U.S. market scaling.

Scale has also allowed Cognyte to drive richer profitability margins. Adjusted EBITDA in Q4 rose 62% y/y to $15.0 million, representing a 14.2% margin that improved 440 bps y/y.

Another critical note here is that, especially relative to other SaaS companies, Cognyte's stock-based compensation is relatively low. The $21.2 million of SBC in FY26 is only 8% of opex and 3% of the company's current outstanding market cap, indicating quite low dilution. Amid Cognyte's transition to meaningfully cheap adjusted EBITDA multiples, this should be another peg in the company's favor.

Key takeaways

Cognyte remains a well-balanced software investment, offering a slew of upside catalysts: new customer growth and healthy billing trends, a niche market that is showcasing momentum rather than headwinds from AI, and continued leverage on the bottom line at cheap multiples of adjusted EBITDA. Stay long here and use the recent dip as a buying opportunity.

data.title: Niu Technologies: Why The Time Has Come To Be Long
data.description:
Niu Technologies (NIU), a manufacturer of urban mobility solutions like electric bicycles, or e-bikes, and scooters, was doing pretty well with the stock rallying prior to the release of the Q4 FY2025 report on March 16, but the selloff that followed afterward wiped all YTD gains and then some. However, there are several reasons why, after all that has happened recently, long NIU is worth taking into consideration at this point.

Why NIU may be at a potential entry point

A previous article from December 2025 written by myself took note of the selloff that had befallen NIU in the preceding weeks, but it also offered reasons why the decline in the stock had in all likelihood run its course. NIU was deemed to be roughly where it ought to be trading in light of expected growth rates at the time, and I accordingly opted to rate NIU a hold in the article.

The chart above shows why the article was correct to think the decline in the stock was over in December because the stock proceeded to reverse course not long thereafter to head north. NIU even managed to put together a fairly substantial rally in the next few months, only to get hit with another selloff in March that brought the stock close to where the previous article thought NIU ought to be.

The main culprit for the decline in recent days can be attributed to the release of the Q4 FY2025 report on March 16, which was met with a 14.8% drop in the stock price. NIU eventually hit a 2026 low of $2.76 per ADS on March 18 before stabilizing. NIU closed at $3.00 per ADS on March 24, which means NIU is now down 1.0% YTD after being way up earlier in the year.

However, a closer look at the chart shows that the stock is not far from a price region where NIU has a past history of turning north after going south. The December 2025 reversal in NIU, for instance, happened after the stock hit a low of $2.89 per ADS before embarking on a rally higher. Going back further shows how the stock hit a 52-week low of $2.73 in April 2025, which was followed by a major move higher.

This is just $0.03 below $2.76, or the low on March 18. The stock has been ticking higher in recent days, which may herald a repeat of what happened twice before in the past 12 months. That is, NIU has tended not to stay long below the $3.00 price point, with recent forays lasting only a short time before the stock moved north. If the pattern holds, this could happen again.

A mixed report from NIU led to a selloff in the stock

The chart posted earlier shows how the stock fell after the release of the most recent, or Q4 FY2025, report. The previous article from December mentioned why there was reason to think that after the strong results NIU had reported earlier in the year, quarterly results were likely to experience a slowdown. As it turned out, the Q4 FY2025 report did show a significant deterioration in results.

As a whole, the recently concluded FY2025 was a year of progress for NIU. FY2025 revenue grew by 31.0% YoY to RMB4,307.87M, or $616.02M, which narrowed GAAP net loss to RMB39.39M, or $5.63M, in FY2025, better than the loss of RMB193.2M in FY2024. On a per-share basis, these numbers translate to net losses of RMB0.49, or $0.07 per ADS in FY2025, better than the loss of RMB2.44 in FY2024. Sales volume increased by 29% to 1.19M units, on top of a 2.1% increase in average selling prices.

But the full-year gains were offset by a slowdown in the final quarter of FY2025. Revenue decreased by 17.4% YoY to RMB676.24M, or $96.70M, which resulted in a GAAP net loss of RMB88.11M, worse than the loss of RMB72.54M a year ago. This translates to losses of RMB1.10, or $0.16 per ADS, worse than the loss of RMB0.91 a year ago. Unit sales declined by 23.8% to 172K, partially offset by a 4% increase in average selling prices.

However, quarterly guidance calls for revenue to grow by 30-50% YoY in Q1 FY2026, or about RMB887-1,023M. In addition, the outlook for FY2026 expects sales volume to increase by 40-60% YoY to 1.7-1.9M units. NIU finished FY2025 with cash and cash equivalents totaling $132.24M, term deposits totaling $18.34M, and short-term investments totaling $8.96M, although partially offset by total debt of $34.9M. There also was another $30.15M locked up in restricted cash. In comparison, NIU's market cap stands at just $234M.

What NIU could be worth in light of the latest outlook

If unit sales grow by 40-60% YoY as the outlook calls for, and we assume average selling prices increase in the single digits as they have recently, at say 3%, and product mix stays about the same, then FY2026 revenue could conceivably grow by 43-63% YoY, which implies FY2026 revenue of $880.91-1,004.11M, using FY2025 revenue of $616.02M as the base. The midpoint would be $942.51M, easily a new record high.

This would almost certainly pull NIU out of the red. Keep in mind that NIU has been working towards this stage in the last few years, with GAAP net loss steadily decreasing in the last three years from $0.49 and $0.33 to the most recent $0.07 per ADS in FY2025, or a total improvement of $0.42 a share. This improvement was enabled by an increase in revenue of almost 65% during these years.

If we then extrapolate into the future and assume FY2026 revenue grows by 53% YoY, FY2026 GAAP net income could improve to an estimated $0.30 per ADS, give or take a few cents, or an improvement of $0.37 per ADS YoY. If we then apply a multiple of 16x, or the average in the sector, then that would yield a stock price of $4.80 per ADS, assuming NIU trades at 16x FY2026 estimated earnings. This is well above where the stock currently trades at.

What could be ahead if NIU keeps growing like it has

If the above estimates are on point, revenue will have grown from $373.6M in FY2023 to $942.5M in FY2026, using the midpoint of the outlook. This translates to a CAGR of 36.13% in three years, or FY2023-2026. Earnings will have improved from a net loss of $0.49 per ADS in FY2023 to a net profit of $0.30 per ADS in FY2026, an increase of $0.79 in three years.

If we project and extrapolate into the future and assume earnings increase by another $0.79 in the next three years, or FY2026-2029, then FY2029 EPADS could hit $1.09. This would translate to a CAGR of 53.73% during this time frame, using FY2026 EPADS of $0.30 as the base, which looks plausible if revenue grows at a CAGR of 36.13% in FY2026-2029.

According to the methodology espoused by Peter Lynch, NIU could be argued to be undervalued because it would have a PEG well below one, or 0.3x to be exact, based on the stock trading at a P/E ratio of 16x and assuming earnings grow at a CAGR of 53.73%. Of course, this is assuming NIU grows in the coming years at rates comparable to those in recent years.

Why recent geopolitical events could be a game changer for NIU

While this is not guaranteed, there is reason to be optimistic about NIU's growth prospects in the near future. This is because recent geopolitical events are very likely to help boost demand for products from NIU. NIU is, after all, a manufacturer of electric two-wheelers, whether electric scooters, bicycles, or motorcycles.

The issue here is that with the huge increase in prices for crude oil and natural gas in many parts of the world as a result of the war in Iran and the disruptions it has caused, particularly in the Strait of Hormuz, consumers have a real incentive to look at the electrical option when it comes to transportation.

All means of transportation relying on fossil fuels like oil and gas will get a lot more expensive if oil and gas are in short supply and prices are high. One way to get out from under this is to replace whatever is used to move around and relies on petrol or whatever fossil fuel is used with something using electric power.

Electric two-wheelers are one alternative, especially since they offer much higher efficiency in terms of cost per mile, and that was true even before the recent rise in oil prices. There are many different types of two-wheelers, and it depends on the exact model and its specs, but many electric scooters from suppliers like NIU could cover a distance of 65 miles, or about 100 km, on a single charge. Some are better, and some are worse than this. This should be enough for most use cases.

This would require about 30 watt-hours per mile, although this again can vary depending on the model, which would translate into a cost of about $0.02 per mile in the U.S., although electricity prices can vary depending on location. Using an electric scooter can be much cheaper to operate than running on gasoline, with potential savings of 80% or more quite possible. These savings could be even greater in other countries, like the European ones where gasoline and/or diesel are much more expensive than in the U.S.

If anyone is hurting from high gas prices and still needs a way to move around, then buying an electric two-wheeler is one way to counter the effect of high oil prices. It may not be an option for everyone, especially since scooters, for example, are not allowed in some places, but it is an option for a lot of people out there, especially in countries where there are already a lot of gasoline-powered two-wheelers. If oil prices stay high, either because the conflict continues and/or production facilities have been damaged and need time to repair, the current environment could power a surge in demand for NIU. Both in China and likely outside of China in particular.

Investor takeaways

NIU continues to be in the red as the company ended the recently concluded FY2025 with another net loss. However, while there was a slowdown towards the end of the fiscal year, NIU has been growing the top line, and the bottom line has improved with losses getting smaller. Furthermore, FY2026 is shaping up to be a year for the record books.

There is a good chance NIU gets out of the red in FY2026 if NIU manages to increase sales volume by 40-60% YoY. The latter looks possible because of how oil prices have increased due to the war in Iran, and it might even turn out to be conservative, depending on how the situation in the Middle East evolves.

There is a much greater incentive to purchase an electric two-wheeler from suppliers like NIU compared to, say, December of last year, when it appeared gas prices would be low because the oil market was expected to be oversupplied in 2026, both by the IEA and OPEC. Yet recent geopolitical events have turned this upside down, and NIU looks to be a beneficiary of this as essentially a supplier of electric two-wheelers.

Now may also be a good time to open positions in NIU, with the stock close to a price level where it has a history of going on rallies on the way higher. The stock fell after NIU reported a slowdown in growth in the last quarter of FY2025, but this looks to be just a temporary speed bump since growth is likely to accelerate from here on out.

A previous article rated NIU a hold in light of growth prospects, but growth prospects for NIU are much higher now than a few months ago, and I am thus bullish on NIU with everything above in mind. Whether it is the strong growth expected this year or the position of the stock close to a good entry point, NIU looks to be in an excellent position to do well this year, with recent world events supporting it. Chances are that is precisely what will happen in 2026.
